sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_time TIMESTAMP, description VARCHAR);

INSERT INTO test_dates (id, event_time, description) VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2022-01-01 10:00:00', 'New Year'), (2, TIMESTAMP '2022-06-01 15:30:00', 'Mid Year'), (3, TIMESTAMP '2023-12-31 23:59:59', 'New Year Eve');
SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL to_microseconds(5) SECOND AS adjusted_time FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, (event_time + INTERVAL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates) DAY + INTERVAL to_microseconds(1) SECOND) AS future_event FROM test_dates WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM test_dates);
SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL (SELECT SUM(LENGTH(description)) FROM test_dates WHERE event_time < TIMESTAMP '2023-01-01 00:00:00') DAY AS time_adjusted FROM test_dates;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY event_time + INTERVAL to_microseconds(5) SECOND) AS event_count FROM test_dates GROUP BY event_time + INTERVAL to_microseconds(5) SECOND;
SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL to_microseconds((SELECT MIN(id) FROM test_dates)) DAY AS min_adjusted_time FROM test_dates HAVING id IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_dates WHERE description IS NULL) DAY + INTERVAL to_microseconds(10) SECOND AS null_sensitive FROM test_dates WHERE description IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE test_intervals (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, duration INTEGER);
INSERT INTO test_intervals (id, duration) VALUES (1, 5), (2, NULL), (3, 0);
SELECT id, to_microseconds(duration) + INTERVAL 1 HOUR AS adjusted_duration FROM test_intervals WHERE duration IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_intervals) + INTERVAL to_microseconds(10) DAY AS count_adjusted FROM test_intervals;
SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL (CASE WHEN id % 2 = 0 THEN to_microseconds(5) ELSE to_microseconds(-5) END) HOUR FROM test_dates ORDER BY adjusted_time;
SELECT id, event_time + INTERVAL (SELECT AVG(COALESCE(duration, 0)) FROM test_intervals) DAY AS avg_adjusted_time FROM test_dates;
